name: Coupa API
what_it_does: Enterprise spend management and procurement platform API covering purchase orders, invoices, contracts, supplier management, and expense reporting for Fortune 500 procurement automation.
use_cases: Automating purchase order creation, routing, and approval workflows
Invoice processing and three-way matching (PO, receipt, invoice)
Supplier onboarding, management, and performance tracking
Contract lifecycle management and compliance monitoring
Expense report submission and policy enforcement
not_for: Small or mid-market businesses — Coupa is designed for enterprise scale with pricing to match
Simple purchase order workflows — overkill for organizations without complex approval hierarchies
Rapid prototyping — Coupa implementation requires significant enterprise configuration
best_when: An agent automates enterprise procurement workflows — routing POs through approval chains, processing invoices, or managing supplier interactions — within an organization already using Coupa.
avoid_when: Your organization is not already on Coupa, or you need a procurement tool at SMB scale and price point.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow supported for server-to-server (agentic) use. Each Coupa instance is a separate tenant with its own OAuth configuration. Scopes map to Coupa business objects (purchasing, invoicing, contracts, etc.). API keys also available for some integrations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise contract pricing — typically $150K-$500K+/yr depending on modules and users. API access is included.
pricing.notes: Coupa is enterprise-only with custom pricing. No self-serve signup or sandbox without a sales engagement. Sandbox environments are provisioned for clients during implementation.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by contract; typically 1000-2000 requests/hr per tenant

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Coupa error responses include HTTP status codes and JSON error details but can be verbose with enterprise-specific error codes that require consulting documentation. Approval workflow errors are particularly opaque.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Purchase order and invoice creation supports idempotency through Coupa's unique identifier pattern, but agents must track external reference IDs carefully. Approval actions are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Each Coupa tenant has a unique base URL — agents must be configured per deployment
Approval workflows have complex state machines; agents must handle pending/rejected/approved states explicitly
OAuth client credentials must be configured by Coupa administrators — not self-service
API versioning (v2) is stable but some endpoints have tenant-specific configurations that can differ
Three-way matching logic (PO/receipt/invoice) requires careful field mapping or invoices get stuck
Supplier portal interactions differ from buyer-side API — separate authentication flows
Enterprise SLA requirements may require dedicated integration middleware

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced across all endpoints. OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow is well-suited for agentic use. FedRAMP authorization demonstrates strong security posture. Tenant isolation is robust. Audit logging is comprehensive for enterprise compliance.
